dependencies: Required to procure certain materials and component parts from supply sources approved by the U.S. Government
Only one supplier may exist for certain components and parts required to manufacture products
Reliance on third parties to provide raw materials, major components and sub-systems
risks: Disruptions and performance issues from suppliers and subcontractors
Unanticipated cost growth for products and services from suppliers
Global supply chain experiencing high levels of disruption causing materials and parts shortages
Delivery delays, labor shortages, and price increases
Raw material shortages including steel, microelectronics and commodity shortages
Supplier challenges impacting ability to provide agreed-upon goods and services in timely, compliant, and cost-effective manner

regulatory_risks: Subject to Federal Acquisition Regulation (FAR) and Defense Federal Acquisition Regulation Supplement (DFARS)
Truth in Negotiations Act compliance
Procurement Integrity Act requirements
False Claims Act exposure
U.S. Cost Accounting Standards (CAS) compliance
International Traffic in Arms Regulations under Arms Export Control Act
Close the Contractor Fraud Loophole Act
Foreign Corrupt Practices Act
Evolving cyber security and data privacy and protection laws and regulations
Environmental laws and regulations (Clean Air Act, Clean Water Act, EPA regulations)
Nuclear operations subject to U.S. Navy, Department of Energy, and U.S. Nuclear Regulatory Commission safety requirements
Federal and state workers' compensation laws
ERISA and Pension Protection Act of 2006 funding requirements
Potential suspension or debarment from government contracts
Changes in DoD business practices and procurement processes
New or revised security and safety requirements for nuclear operations
Climate change and environmental regulations including greenhouse gas emissions, carbon pricing, energy taxes
Mandatory disclosure obligations related to cybersecurity events
Changes in tax laws and regulations
Import-export and technology transfer laws
Anti-corruption laws
Anti-boycott laws

sanction_risks: Military conflicts between Russia and Ukraine resulting in sanctions
Increased security assistance to Ukraine and Israel impacting defense budgetary priorities
Tensions with China affecting global market for defense products
Heightened cyber and security risks from global conflicts
